notes: SHIP v1.14.252 — TWO JOHN RULINGS (2026-07-14), recorded in INTEGRATION-STATE §23 before the context compact and shipped together here. THREE EDITS, no markup, no CSS, no new function. ── RULING 1 · q3400-ra = 2U. The single largest block of height-unknown devices on either master: 448 SPARKS hosts, hatched since .238. Typed as an InfiniBand switch in .247; this settles the HEIGHT and STOPS THE HATCH. ⭐ EVIDENCE IS JOHN'S WORD AND NOTHING ELSE, AND THAT IS RECORDED IN THE CODE: unlike the HGX 6U or net-6x100g-02 rulings, the master's own geometry does NOT determine this one — SPARKS places these at a UNIFORM 5U pitch, which only BOUNDS the chassis at <=5U. 2U sits INSIDE that bound (a 3U service gap per pitch is unremarkable), so the ruling is CONSISTENT with the floor, but the floor could never have told us 2U on its own. Logged as owner-evidence, not geometry-evidence, so a future import with a conflicting pitch cannot be silently overruled by it. MODEL-EXACT, boundary = the negated class `[^a-z0-9-]`, NOT \b (§18 law: \b matches on BOTH sides of a hyphen, so \bq3400-ra\b would also swallow q3400-ra-x). The regex is CHARACTER-IDENTICAL to the .247 TYPE rule on purpose — the type and the height must agree on exactly which hosts they mean. Also removed q3400-ra from the MASTER_U_TABLE 'DELIBERATELY ABSENT' list, which would otherwise still be claiming it needs a ruling. ── RULING 2 · type `unknown` GOLD -> MAGENTA #ff2bd6. Kills the pdu/unknown collision (both were #ffcb45, so a PDU spine and an UNKNOWN spine were indistinguishable BY COLOUR ALONE — label and flag differed, colour did not). PDU KEEPS THE GOLD; only `unknown` moves. #ff2bd6 is the app's EXISTING --mag brand token (Design Law 5), not a new colour. ⭐ JOHN ASKED FOR 'spine, legend, detail dots together' AND THE CODE ALREADY GUARANTEES IT: TYPE_COLOR.unknown is the SINGLE source feeding all three — the tray SPINE (TYPE_COLOR[sl.type], the 5px lane bar), the detail-row DOT (.type-dot) and the search-result LABEL. ONE value, so they move in lockstep and CANNOT drift apart. There is no separate legend element in the Forge tray; the TLABEL text beside each name is var(--dim) grey for EVERY type, i.e. not a colour key, so it is untouched (colouring it would change all 9 types, which John did not rule). ⛔ THE TRAP I DID NOT WALK INTO, AND THE COMMENT NOW GUARDS IT: the gold at the tray's hazard paint (the diagonal hatch, the ⚠ name text, the hard edge) is NOT type-unknown — it is gated on `unk` = sl.hgtUnknown, the HEIGHT hazard (see the flagged split: sl.conflict || sl.overflow || sl.hgtUnknown). A naive find-and-replace on #ffcb45 would have recoloured it and COLLAPSED THE VERY DISTINCTION .246/.247 EXIST TO PROTECT: type-unknown draws SOLID + TO SCALE because the U-span IS true, while the hazard means DO NOT TRUST THIS GEOMETRY. That gold STAYS. Equally untouched: the --gold token, the RACK MAP / KNOW rf-card accents, BURNDOWN, .va-i-type, .ctag b. ── GUARD (both real masters replayed through the SHIPPED functions vs the .251 baseline): EXACTLY 448 hosts change height, ALL of them q3400-ra, {u:null,known:false} -> {u:2,known:true}. ZERO other model's height moves. ZERO TYPE DRIFT on either master (0 of 6490 — this ship must not retype a single host, and it does not). DFW02 COMPLETELY UNCHANGED. Boundary probes PASS: q3400-ra -> 2U; q3400-ra-x, xq3400-ra, q3400-rb, q3400, mqm8700-q3400-ra -> ALL still unknown. COLOUR INVARIANTS (.246 class) GREEN: every _TMAP value resolves to a defined TLABEL AND TYPE_COLOR (a miss here prints the literal word 'undefined' on a tray), and NO TWO TYPES NOW SHARE A COLOUR — the first time that has been true. ── HATCH COUNT: SPARKS 856 -> 408 hatched hosts (q3400-ra was more than half of what remained). DFW02 unchanged at 61. ⚠️ NEW ADJACENCY, FLAGGED HONESTLY, NOT HIDDEN: `unknown` #ff2bd6 (magenta) now sits beside `storage` #ff7bd0 (pink) — distinct hex, SAME HUE FAMILY. The gold collision is genuinely dead, but this may have traded it for a magenta/pink pair. Needs John's eye on a real tray; it is a one-token change if he dislikes it. Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS · CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship) · CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0) · three-stamp lockstep .251 -> .252. ?legacy=1 UNAFFECTED: MASTER_U_TABLE/master_nodeHeightInfo are pure data+classifier, TYPE_COLOR is display-only inside forge3d_render; no markup, no gating, no CSS. deploy_classifyDevice (the PERSISTED EDP/CSV path) untouched, so no seeded deployment record changes shape. BATCH: opens at 2 (.251 MEDIA CONV + .252). Device-verify on .251 is STILL OWED and unwalked — 'i looked' was an owner ACK, not an itemized pass. ── DEVICE-VERIFY (John): [.252-A · q3400-ra] SPARKS, any q3400-ra rack (448 hosts, e.g. the s3 IB fabric) — the rows now draw SOLID, 2U TO SCALE, NO gold hatch, NO '⚠ MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN'. Spine stays VIOLET (switch, from .247) — the type did not change. Confirm no rack suddenly reports a U-COLLISION that it did not have at .251 (2U is inside the 5U pitch, so it must not; if one appears, that rack's master geometry is the suspect, not this rule). [.252-B · magenta] Any tray with an UNKNOWN-type host — the spine, the detail-row dot and the search-result label all read MAGENTA together; a PDU beside it still reads GOLD and is now unmistakably a different device. On DFW02 the empty-model row @c1:001:38 is the one to look at. [.252-C · the line that must NOT have moved] A HEIGHT-UNKNOWN host (SPARKS gpu-b40-02 x160, cpu-gp2-* x165, or fs-media-converter-chassis x5) still carries the GOLD hazard hatch + '⚠ MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN'. Gold hazard and magenta type are now two DIFFERENT signals on screen — that is the whole point. [.252-D] Nothing prints 'undefined'. RACKED/PENDING counts unchanged. ── STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING: DFW02's one empty-model row @c1:001:38 (the last unknown-TYPE host on either master) · the HEIGHT of fs-media-converter-chassis (5) · and the SPARKS height remainder now visible at the top of the hatch list: gpu-b40-02 (160), cpu-gp2-01 (90), cpu-gp2-08 (75), inf-med-01 (60), om2216-c14 (16) · whether the new magenta-vs-storage-pink adjacency bothers him.
